common.disclaimer_clear: This action will permanently delete all medication records, lab records, and personalized settings, and cannot be undone. It is recommended to export a data backup before proceeding.
common.model_explanation_desc: Understand the scientific principles of pharmacokinetic models
common.github_repo_desc: The source code repository for this project
common.original_author_desc: Tribute to the contribution of the original author SmirnovaOyama
common.disclaimer_1: 1. This software is for reference only. All data provided (including but not limited to estimated blood concentration values) are based on pharmacokinetic mathematical models and cannot be used as a basis for medical diagnosis, treatment, or medication.
common.disclaimer_2: 2. Pharmacokinetics are greatly affected by individual differences (such as genes, age, health status, lifestyle, etc.), and there may be significant deviations between model estimates and actual blood test results.
common.disclaimer_3: 3. It is strictly forbidden to adjust medication regimens based on the estimated data of this software. Any medication adjustment must be carried out under the guidance of a professional physician, combined with actual clinical manifestations and hospital blood test reports.
common.disclaimer_4: 4. The developer is not responsible for any direct or indirect consequences arising from the use of this software or the information it provides.

notice_modal.title: Important Notice
notice_modal.p1: Recently, some users have reported abnormal deviations in estimated data after calibration based on blood test results.
notice_modal.p2: Please understand: This software provides theoretical estimates based on pharmacokinetic models and cannot replace actual blood tests. Absorption and metabolism can vary even for the same individual over time.
notice_modal.p3: The only way to accurately know your blood concentration is to have a blood test at a hospital. Always rely on hospital test reports as the basis for adjusting medication, and do not rely solely on the estimates of this software.

records.title: Medication Records
records.ref_range: Reference: Low ≤ 1.5 mg/d · Medium ≤ 3 mg/d · High ≤ 6 mg/d · Very High ≤ 9 mg/d
records.usage_warning_desc: Do not use if you are at high risk for breast cancer or have severe liver disorders!
records.injection_warning: Injection: Do not inject if you don't know how!
records.ev_dosage: EV: 5 mg every 5-7 days / 10 mg every 1-2 weeks
records.ec_dosage: EC: 5-6 mg every 7 days / 10-12 mg every 14 days
records.note_1: Open ampoules correctly to avoid glass cuts or shards in the medication.
records.note_2: Do not inject in the same spot repeatedly.
records.note_3: Avoid blood vessels, risk of embolism!
records.note_4: Avoid nerves, inject in safe areas.
records.note_5: Use 18G (pink) for drawing and 23G/25G (blue) for injection.
records.note_6: Fast needle insertion and new needles for injection help reduce pain.
records.note_7: Suggested sites: vastus lateralis (thigh) or deltoid (arm).
records.note_8: If you feel sharp pain or see blood return, withdraw immediately and change site.
records.note_9: Minor bleeding after injection is normal, don't panic.

calibration.title: Lab Records (Calibration)
calibration.why_desc: By entering your E2 values from hospital tests, the system can automatically calibrate the simulation curve based on your personal metabolism for more accurate predictions.
